name: SIG 2026 (Standardized Information Gathering)
description: The Shared Assessments SIG questionnaire is the industry gold standard for third-party risk assessment, covering 21 risk domains with comprehensive controls for evaluating vendor security posture. Updated for 2026 with enhanced AI/ML, cloud security, and supply chain risk domains.

categories.name: Enterprise Risk Management
categories.description: Controls related to organizational risk management strategy, governance, and oversight.

controls.name: Risk Management Program
controls.description: Organization maintains a formal enterprise risk management program.

requirements.description: A formal enterprise risk management program exists that identifies, assesses, and manages risks across the organization.
requirements.guidance: Request risk management policy and program documentation. Verify executive sponsorship and board oversight.

requirements.description: Risk assessments are performed at least annually and when significant changes occur.
requirements.guidance: Review risk assessment schedule and recent assessment reports.

requirements.description: Risk tolerance levels are defined and approved by executive management.
requirements.guidance: Examine risk appetite statements and tolerance thresholds.

controls.name: Third-Party Risk Management
controls.description: Organization has processes to identify, assess, and manage risks from third parties.

requirements.description: A third-party risk management program exists with defined policies and procedures.
requirements.guidance: Request TPRM policy and vendor management procedures.

requirements.description: Vendors are categorized by risk level based on data access and criticality.
requirements.guidance: Review vendor tiering methodology and classification criteria.

requirements.description: Due diligence is performed on vendors prior to engagement and periodically thereafter.
requirements.guidance: Examine vendor onboarding checklists and reassessment schedules.

categories.name: Security Policy
categories.description: Information security policies and their management.

controls.name: Information Security Policy
controls.description: Organization maintains comprehensive information security policies.

requirements.description: An information security policy approved by management is published and communicated to employees.
requirements.guidance: Request current information security policy. Verify approval date and communication methods.

requirements.description: Policies are reviewed and updated at least annually or when significant changes occur.
requirements.guidance: Review policy revision history and change management records.

requirements.description: Policy exceptions require documented approval and are tracked.
requirements.guidance: Examine exception request process and tracking mechanisms.

categories.name: Organizational Security
categories.description: Security organization structure, roles, and responsibilities.

controls.name: Security Organization
controls.description: Clear organizational structure for information security management.

requirements.description: A CISO or equivalent role exists with responsibility for information security.
requirements.guidance: Identify the security leadership structure and reporting lines.

requirements.description: Security roles and responsibilities are clearly defined and documented.
requirements.guidance: Review job descriptions and RACI matrices for security functions.

requirements.description: Adequate resources are allocated to support the security program.
requirements.guidance: Examine security team size, budget allocation, and tool investments.

controls.name: Segregation of Duties
controls.description: Conflicting duties and areas of responsibility are segregated.

requirements.description: Duties are segregated to reduce opportunities for unauthorized modification or misuse.
requirements.guidance: Review access control matrices and approval workflows.

categories.name: Asset Management
categories.description: Identification and classification of information assets.

controls.name: Asset Inventory
controls.description: Organization maintains an inventory of information assets.

requirements.description: A complete inventory of information assets is maintained and regularly updated.
requirements.guidance: Request asset inventory reports and update frequency.

requirements.description: Asset owners are assigned and accountable for asset protection.
requirements.guidance: Review asset ownership assignments and accountability structures.

controls.name: Data Classification
controls.description: Information is classified according to sensitivity and criticality.

requirements.description: A data classification scheme exists with defined handling requirements.
requirements.guidance: Request data classification policy and handling guidelines.

requirements.description: Data is labeled according to its classification level.
requirements.guidance: Examine data labeling practices and enforcement mechanisms.

controls.name: Background Verification
controls.description: Background checks are performed on personnel.

requirements.description: Background checks are performed on all employees prior to employment.
requirements.guidance: Review background check policy and screening procedures.

requirements.description: Periodic re-verification is performed for personnel with elevated access.
requirements.guidance: Examine re-screening schedules for sensitive positions.

controls.name: Security Awareness Training
controls.description: Personnel receive security awareness training.

requirements.description: Security awareness training is provided to all employees upon hire and annually.
requirements.guidance: Request training curriculum and completion records.

requirements.description: Role-based security training is provided for personnel with specialized functions.
requirements.guidance: Review specialized training programs for developers, admins, etc.

requirements.description: Phishing simulations or tests are conducted regularly.
requirements.guidance: Examine phishing test results and remediation actions.

controls.name: Termination Process
controls.description: Secure offboarding procedures exist.

requirements.description: Access is revoked promptly upon termination or role change.
requirements.guidance: Review termination procedures and access revocation timelines.

controls.name: Physical Access Control
controls.description: Physical access to facilities is controlled.

requirements.description: Physical access to sensitive areas requires authentication (badge, biometric, etc.).
requirements.guidance: Review physical access control systems and entry logs.

requirements.description: Visitor access is controlled and logged.
requirements.guidance: Examine visitor management procedures and logs.

controls.name: Data Center Security
controls.description: Data center facilities have appropriate security controls.

requirements.description: Data centers have multiple layers of physical security.
requirements.guidance: Review data center security documentation or SOC reports.

requirements.description: Environmental controls (HVAC, fire suppression) are in place.
requirements.guidance: Examine environmental monitoring and protection systems.

controls.name: Change Management
controls.description: Changes to systems are controlled.

requirements.description: A formal change management process exists for all system changes.
requirements.guidance: Request change management policy and review change tickets.

requirements.description: Changes are tested in non-production before deployment.
requirements.guidance: Examine testing procedures and approval workflows.

requirements.description: Emergency changes follow an expedited but documented process.
requirements.guidance: Review emergency change procedures and recent examples.

controls.name: Capacity Management
controls.description: System capacity is monitored and managed.

requirements.description: System capacity is monitored and future needs are projected.
requirements.guidance: Review capacity monitoring tools and planning documents.

controls.name: Backup and Recovery
controls.description: Data backup and recovery procedures.

requirements.description: Critical data is backed up regularly according to defined schedules.
requirements.guidance: Request backup policy and schedule documentation.

requirements.description: Backup restoration is tested periodically.
requirements.guidance: Review backup restoration test results and frequency.

requirements.description: Backups are stored securely, including offsite or cloud storage.
requirements.guidance: Examine backup storage locations and encryption.

categories.name: Access Control
categories.description: Logical access control to systems and data.

controls.name: Access Control Policy
controls.description: Access to systems is controlled based on business needs.

requirements.description: Access is granted based on the principle of least privilege.
requirements.guidance: Review access provisioning procedures and role definitions.

requirements.description: Access rights are reviewed periodically (at least quarterly for privileged access).
requirements.guidance: Examine access review procedures and completion records.

controls.name: User Authentication
controls.description: Strong authentication mechanisms are implemented.

requirements.description: Multi-factor authentication is required for remote access and administrative functions.
requirements.guidance: Review MFA implementation and coverage.

requirements.description: Password policies enforce complexity, length, and rotation requirements.
requirements.guidance: Request password policy and technical enforcement settings.

requirements.description: Account lockout is enforced after failed authentication attempts.
requirements.guidance: Examine lockout thresholds and duration settings.

controls.name: Privileged Access Management
controls.description: Elevated access is tightly controlled.

requirements.description: Administrative access requires additional approval and is logged.
requirements.guidance: Review privileged access request and approval process.

requirements.description: Privileged credentials are managed through a PAM solution or equivalent.
requirements.guidance: Examine privileged access management tools and procedures.

categories.name: Application Security
categories.description: Security in application development and maintenance.

controls.name: Secure Development
controls.description: Applications are developed securely.

requirements.description: A secure software development lifecycle (SSDLC) is followed.
requirements.guidance: Request SDLC documentation and security integration points.

requirements.description: Developers receive secure coding training.
requirements.guidance: Review developer training curriculum and completion.

requirements.description: Code reviews include security considerations.
requirements.guidance: Examine code review procedures and security checklists.

controls.name: Application Testing
controls.description: Applications are tested for security vulnerabilities.

requirements.description: Static application security testing (SAST) is performed.
requirements.guidance: Review SAST tool configuration and scan results.

requirements.description: Dynamic application security testing (DAST) is performed.
requirements.guidance: Examine DAST procedures and findings.

requirements.description: Penetration testing is performed at least annually.
requirements.guidance: Request penetration test reports and remediation evidence.

controls.name: Open Source Security
controls.description: Open source and third-party components are managed.

requirements.description: Software composition analysis (SCA) is performed to identify vulnerabilities in dependencies.
requirements.guidance: Review SCA tool implementation and findings.

requirements.description: A software bill of materials (SBOM) is maintained.
requirements.guidance: Request SBOM documentation and update procedures.

controls.name: Incident Response
controls.description: A formal incident response capability exists.

requirements.description: An incident response plan is documented and approved.
requirements.guidance: Request incident response plan and review approval.

requirements.description: Incident response roles and responsibilities are defined.
requirements.guidance: Review IRT structure and contact information.

requirements.description: Incident response procedures are tested at least annually.
requirements.guidance: Examine tabletop exercises and drill results.

controls.name: Security Monitoring
controls.description: Security events are monitored and analyzed.

requirements.description: Security information and event management (SIEM) is implemented.
requirements.guidance: Review SIEM configuration and log sources.

requirements.description: Security alerts are triaged and investigated 24/7.
requirements.guidance: Examine SOC procedures and coverage model.

controls.name: Incident Notification
controls.description: Affected parties are notified of security incidents.

requirements.description: Customer notification procedures exist for security incidents.
requirements.guidance: Review notification procedures and SLA commitments.

requirements.description: Regulatory notification requirements are tracked and met.
requirements.guidance: Examine regulatory notification tracking and compliance.

categories.name: Business Continuity
categories.description: Business continuity and disaster recovery planning.

controls.name: BCP/DRP
controls.description: Business continuity and disaster recovery plans exist.

requirements.description: Business impact analysis is performed to identify critical functions.
requirements.guidance: Request BIA documentation and results.

requirements.description: Recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO) are defined.
requirements.guidance: Review RTO/RPO definitions for critical systems.

requirements.description: DR plans are tested at least annually.
requirements.guidance: Examine DR test results and lessons learned.

controls.name: Regulatory Compliance
controls.description: Applicable regulatory requirements are identified and met.

requirements.description: Applicable laws and regulations are identified and tracked.
requirements.guidance: Request regulatory inventory and compliance tracking.

requirements.description: Compliance is validated through audits or assessments.
requirements.guidance: Review compliance audit results and certifications.

controls.name: Audit Support
controls.description: Audit requirements are supported.

requirements.description: Audit logs are retained according to regulatory and business requirements.
requirements.guidance: Examine log retention policies and implementation.

requirements.description: Customers can request audit rights per contractual terms.
requirements.guidance: Review audit rights provisions in contracts.

controls.name: Privacy Program
controls.description: A privacy program exists to protect personal information.

requirements.description: A privacy policy exists and is communicated to data subjects.
requirements.guidance: Request privacy policy and notice documentation.

requirements.description: Privacy impact assessments are performed for new processing activities.
requirements.guidance: Review PIA process and recent assessments.

requirements.description: Data subject rights (access, deletion, portability) can be fulfilled.
requirements.guidance: Examine data subject request procedures and fulfillment.

controls.name: Data Protection
controls.description: Personal data is protected appropriately.

requirements.description: Personal data is encrypted at rest and in transit.
requirements.guidance: Review encryption standards for personal data.

requirements.description: Data minimization principles are applied.
requirements.guidance: Examine data collection practices and retention limits.

requirements.description: Cross-border data transfers comply with applicable requirements.
requirements.guidance: Review data transfer mechanisms (SCCs, adequacy, etc.).

controls.name: Network Protection
controls.description: Networks are protected from unauthorized access.

requirements.description: Firewalls protect network boundaries.
requirements.guidance: Review firewall architecture and rule sets.

requirements.description: Network segmentation separates sensitive systems.
requirements.guidance: Examine network diagrams and segmentation strategy.

requirements.description: Intrusion detection/prevention systems are implemented.
requirements.guidance: Review IDS/IPS deployment and monitoring.

controls.name: Remote Access
controls.description: Remote access is secured.

requirements.description: VPN or zero-trust network access is required for remote connections.
requirements.guidance: Review remote access architecture and requirements.

requirements.description: Remote access sessions are logged and monitored.
requirements.guidance: Examine remote access logging and review procedures.

categories.name: Cloud Security
categories.description: Security controls for cloud computing environments.

controls.name: Cloud Governance
controls.description: Cloud usage is governed and controlled.

requirements.description: Cloud service providers are assessed for security prior to use.
requirements.guidance: Review CSP assessment process and vendor reviews.

requirements.description: Cloud security responsibilities are clearly defined (shared responsibility model).
requirements.guidance: Examine shared responsibility documentation.

controls.name: Cloud Configuration
controls.description: Cloud resources are securely configured.

requirements.description: Cloud security posture management (CSPM) tools monitor configuration.
requirements.guidance: Review CSPM implementation and findings.

requirements.description: Infrastructure as code is used with security scanning.
requirements.guidance: Examine IaC practices and security scanning integration.

requirements.description: Cloud access is controlled through identity federation where possible.
requirements.guidance: Review identity federation and access management.

controls.name: Endpoint Protection
controls.description: Endpoints are protected from malware and threats.

requirements.description: EDR/XDR solutions are deployed on all endpoints.
requirements.guidance: Review endpoint protection deployment and coverage.

requirements.description: Endpoints are hardened according to security baselines.
requirements.guidance: Examine hardening standards and compliance.

requirements.description: Full disk encryption is enabled on all endpoints.
requirements.guidance: Review encryption deployment and management.

controls.name: Mobile Device Management
controls.description: Mobile devices are managed securely.

requirements.description: Mobile device management (MDM) is implemented for corporate devices.
requirements.guidance: Review MDM solution and enrollment.

requirements.description: BYOD policies define security requirements for personal devices.
requirements.guidance: Examine BYOD policy and controls.

controls.name: Encryption Standards
controls.description: Strong cryptography is used appropriately.

requirements.description: Industry-standard encryption algorithms are used (AES-256, RSA-2048+, etc.).
requirements.guidance: Review encryption standards and algorithm inventory.

requirements.description: TLS 1.2 or higher is required for data in transit.
requirements.guidance: Examine TLS configuration and certificate management.

controls.name: Key Management
controls.description: Cryptographic keys are managed securely.

requirements.description: Key management procedures are documented and followed.
requirements.guidance: Request key management procedures and lifecycle.

requirements.description: Keys are stored in hardware security modules (HSMs) or equivalent.
requirements.guidance: Review key storage mechanisms and protection.

requirements.description: Key rotation is performed according to defined schedules.
requirements.guidance: Examine key rotation procedures and evidence.

controls.name: Vulnerability Scanning
controls.description: Systems are scanned for vulnerabilities.

requirements.description: Vulnerability scans are performed at least monthly on all systems.
requirements.guidance: Review vulnerability scanning schedule and coverage.

requirements.description: Critical vulnerabilities are remediated within defined SLAs.
requirements.guidance: Examine remediation SLAs and compliance metrics.

controls.name: Patch Management
controls.description: Systems are patched promptly.

requirements.description: Critical patches are applied within 14 days of release.
requirements.guidance: Review patch management policy and compliance.

requirements.description: Patching is validated through scanning or verification.
requirements.guidance: Examine patch verification procedures.

categories.name: Supply Chain Security
categories.description: Security of the supply chain and software supply chain.

controls.name: Software Supply Chain
controls.description: Software supply chain risks are managed.

requirements.description: Software provenance is verified for critical components.
requirements.guidance: Review software verification procedures.

requirements.description: CI/CD pipelines are secured with access controls and signing.
requirements.guidance: Examine pipeline security controls and artifact signing.

requirements.description: Container images are scanned and signed before deployment.
requirements.guidance: Review container security practices.

controls.name: Fourth-Party Risk
controls.description: Risks from vendors' vendors are considered.

requirements.description: Critical subcontractors are identified and assessed.
requirements.guidance: Request fourth-party inventory and assessment approach.

requirements.description: Contractual flow-down requirements exist for security.
requirements.guidance: Review contract terms for subcontractor requirements.

categories.name: AI and Machine Learning Security
categories.description: Security controls specific to AI/ML systems (2026 addition).

controls.name: AI Governance
controls.description: AI systems are governed appropriately.

requirements.description: AI/ML usage is inventoried and risk-assessed.
requirements.guidance: Request AI system inventory and risk assessments.

requirements.description: Human oversight exists for critical AI decisions.
requirements.guidance: Review human-in-the-loop requirements and implementation.

requirements.description: AI model decisions are explainable where required.
requirements.guidance: Examine explainability capabilities and documentation.

controls.name: AI Security
controls.description: AI systems are secured against specific threats.

requirements.description: Training data is validated for quality and bias.
requirements.guidance: Review data validation and bias testing procedures.

requirements.description: Model integrity is protected against tampering.
requirements.guidance: Examine model protection and versioning controls.

requirements.description: Prompt injection and adversarial attacks are mitigated.
requirements.guidance: Review input validation and attack mitigation for LLMs.

requirements.description: AI outputs are monitored for harmful or unexpected content.
requirements.guidance: Examine output monitoring and filtering capabilities.

controls.name: AI Privacy
controls.description: Privacy is protected in AI systems.

requirements.description: Personal data used for training complies with privacy requirements.
requirements.guidance: Review training data sources and consent mechanisms.

requirements.description: Model outputs do not leak training data (membership inference protection).
requirements.guidance: Examine privacy-preserving techniques and testing.

controls.name: Operational Resilience
controls.description: Operations can withstand and recover from disruptions.

requirements.description: Critical services are designed for high availability.
requirements.guidance: Review architecture for redundancy and failover.

requirements.description: Resilience testing (chaos engineering) is performed.
requirements.guidance: Examine resilience testing practices and results.

requirements.description: Dependencies on third parties are identified and alternatives exist.
requirements.guidance: Review dependency mapping and contingency plans.
